newsArchive.headline: LET'S FRY 'EM
newsArchive.body:
Green Bay, WI - Perhaps parents can rest easier tonight. The authorities have apprehended their primary suspect in the String of brutal child killings that has kept everyone in the area on edge, according to a spokesperson for the police department here.

Jim Ed Kissinger was detained yesterday afternoon, reportedly in possession of a child's clothing stained with DNA evidence. Over twenty children in the past two years have gone missing, only to turn up later dead and sexually mutilated. Sources say that the police got a break in the case when a blood trail was found on a road that led them to the suspect's car trunk.

The district attorney's office has already repeatedly said it will be seeking the death penalty in this case.

newsArchive.headline: CLINIC MURDER
newsArchive.body:
Oxnard, CA - A doctor that routinely performed semi-legal abortions was ruthlessly gunned down outside of the Cuomo Clinic yesterday. Dr. Juliette Souter was walking to her car when, according to police reports, shots were fired from a nearby vehicle. She was hit 13 times and died immediately in the parking lot. The suspected shooter, Gustav Quilico, is in custody.

Witnesses report that Quilico remained at the scene after the shooting, screaming verses of the Bible at the stunned onlookers. Someone called the police on a cellphone and they arrived shortly thereafter. Quilico surrendered without a struggle, reportedly saying that God's work had been completed.

Dr. Souter is survived by her husband and two children.

newsArchive.headline: FM OBSCENITY

Although László Zapata later apologized, the FCC received several hundred complaints from irate listeners from all over the state. A spokesperson for the FCC stated that the incident is under investigation.

newsArchive.headline: JUSTICE AMOK
newsArchive.body:
York, PA - The conviction of confessed serial killer Samira Sophia Weller was overturned by a federal judge yesterday. Judge Alphonse Dukakis of the notoriously liberal court of appeals here made the decision based on his general feelings about police corruption, despite the confession of Weller, which even Judge Dukakis grants was not coerced in any way.

Ten years ago, Weller was convicted of the now-infamous Probst slayings. After an intensive manhunt, Weller was found with the murder weapon covered in the victims' blood. Weller confessed and was sentenced to life, saying "Thank you for saving me from myself. If I were to be released, I would surely kill again."

A spokesperson for the district attorney has stated that the case will not be retried, due to the current economic doldrums that have left the state completely strapped for cash.

newsArchive.headline: FINALLY GONE
newsArchive.body:
Rochester, NY - The nationwide manhunt is over after authorities finally caught Maximilian Kang, an illegal immigrant who captured national attention after he was seen dunking on a white guy in a pickup game in a viral video.

"We got him, but we all know this bastard should never have been here in the first place. Criminals like this are exactly why we need stricter immigration laws," said Congressman Grammer. "We can't continue to allow our immigration laws to be flouted by these sick monsters while our children suffer."

Local residents have expressed growing concern about the impact of immigration on their community, with many calling for new laws to help keep foreign criminals from lowering wages.

newsArchive.headline: DODGED BULLET
newsArchive.body:
Washington, DC - The CIA announced yesterday that it has averted a terror attack that would have occurred on American soil.

According to a spokesperson for the agency, outcast goths from a suburban high school planned to assassinate the president. However, intelligence garnered from deep within the mysterious terrorist organization allowed the plot to be foiled just days before it was to occur.

The spokesperson further stated, "I won't compromise our sources and methods, but let me just say that we are grateful to the Congress and this Administration for providing us with the tools we need to neutralize these enemies of civilization before they can destroy American families. However, let me also say that there's more that needs to be done. The Head of the Agency will be sending a request to Congress for what we feel are the essential tools for combating terrorism in this new age."

newsArchive.headline: HOSTAGE SLAIN
newsArchive.body:
Kent, WA - The hostage crisis at the Hayashi Correctional Facility ended tragically yesterday with the death of both the prison guard being held hostage and his captor.

Two weeks ago, convicted rapist Charlene Muskhelishvili, an inmate at Hayashi, overpowered Ralf Vulcar and barricaded herself with the guard in a prison tower. Authorities locked down the prison and attempted to negotiate by phone for 14 days, but talks were cut short when Charlene Muskhelishvili reportedly screamed into the receiver, "Why the f*ck am I talkin' to you? I'd rather kill this pig."

The tower was breached in an attempt to reach the hostage, but Muskhelishvili had already hit all 36 pressure points of death on the guard. The prisoner was beaten to death while "resisting capture", according to a prison spokesperson.

newsArchive.headline: CRIME OF HATE
newsArchive.body:
Spartanburg, SC - Francine Beaumont, a trans woman, was dragged to death behind a pickup truck here yesterday. A police spokesperson reported that four suspects were apprehended after a high speed chase. Their names have not yet been released.

Witnesses of the freeway chase described the pickup of the alleged murderers swerving wildly, throwing beer bottles at the pursuing police cruisers. The chase ended when the suspects were caught in traffic, at which point they were taken into custody. Nobody was seriously injured during the pursuit.

Authorities have stated that they will vigorously prosecute this case as a hate crime, due to the aggravated nature of the offense.
